name: Kettlebell Front Rack Squat
resistance_profile: Front-loaded squat that keeps the torso upright and loads the quads deep in the bottom stretch.
cues: Sit straight down between the knees
Bells pinned to the forearms, brace hard
execution_steps: Clean two kettlebells to the front rack so the bells rest on the outside of the forearms with elbows tucked in front of the ribs.
Set your feet about shoulder-width, toes slightly out, and brace your core.
Keeping the torso tall and elbows up, sit straight down between your knees to full depth.
Feel the stretch at the bottom, then drive through the whole foot to stand.
Keep the bells pinned to the forearms throughout, then lower them under control after the set.
good_when: You want an upright, quad-biased squat without a barbell
Training with kettlebells at home or in a small space
You want added core and upper-back demand alongside quad work
bad_when: You lack the wrist and shoulder mobility for a comfortable front rack
You need loads heavier than your available kettlebells allow
common_errors: Elbows dropping so the torso folds forward
Letting the bells pull you onto your toes
progressions: Use heavier kettlebells or add reps
Add a pause in the bottom position
regressions: Use a single kettlebell in the goblet position
Reduce depth to a comfortable range
